mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, it signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns are negative in greater than 50 percent of co-expansion periods over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects resilient manufacturing output that offsets rate hike headwinds, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles are followed by negative 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases, or the signal IC turns negative when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings growth, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print above 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4 percent, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market without recessionary risk, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 50 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals a leading downturn in construction and related consumer durables, prompting immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases, or the signal fails to predict direction during late-cycle phases.
